reportTitle: Council Brief v5.3 — Upper-Band Calibration Test Report
testMethod: Test 6 × 3 runs + regression tests (1, 5, 7) on production API.

summary.improvement: Scores improved (v5.2: 67 → v5.3 avg: 70) but still below 75 threshold

test6Runs.verdictReasoning: Promising not strong because while traction is solid (14/20), the company needs to prove it can scale beyond current organic growth and navigate complex multi-market expansion
test6Runs.note: 2 pts short of strong. Defensibility 12 ≥ 11 gate — passed. But total 73 < 75.

test6Runs.verdictReasoning: Promising not strong because while traction and team are excellent, defensibility remains unproven against well-funded global competitors
test6Runs.note: 3 pts short. Defensibility 11 = exact gate minimum. Timing 12 still conservative.

test6Runs.verdictReasoning: Promising not strong because $50K MRR with 200 merchants suggests $250 ARPU which may not be sustainable
test6Runs.note: Worst run. Traction 12 < 14 gate — blocked even if score were 75+. Defensibility 10 < 11 gate — also blocked.

test6Analysis.problem: Rubric changes helped (+3 avg) but not enough. Model still conservative on defensibility/timing. Score 73 was closest — 2 pts short.
test6Analysis.rootCause: Model treats $50K MRR in competitive market as 'promising but unproven' rather than 'strong signal'. The anti-competition instruction helped (defensibility 9→11 avg) but timing improvement was minimal (10→12.7 avg).
options: Option A: Lower strong threshold from 75 to 72. Risk: may let marginal cases through.
Option B: Add explicit scoring instruction: '$50K+ MRR with 15%+ MoM growth = traction 16+'. Forces higher traction score which pushes total over 75.
Option C: Accept that this case is legitimately 'high promising' and adjust expected verdict to promising. Strong should require clearer defensibility proof.
Option D: Add a 'momentum bonus' — if traction >= 14 AND team >= 16 AND no penalties, add +3 bonus. Creates explicit upward pull.

regressionTests.idea: Uber for laundry, solo, no funding
regressionTests.note: Still weak. Score dropped 20→10 (timing 4→18 variance but 3 penalties still force weak).
Still promising. Traction 12 < 14 blocks strong. No inflation.
Still weak. 3 penalties applied. baseScore 24, councilScore 0.

v5.3Impact.defensibilityChange: v5.2: 9 → v5.3 avg: 11 (+2). Rubric change helped.
v5.3Impact.totalScoreChange: v5.2: 67 → v5.3 avg: 70 (+3). Not enough for 75.
v5.3Impact.gatesPassed: Run 1: all gates passed except score < 75. Run 2: all gates passed except score < 75. Run 3: traction AND defensibility gates failed.
v5.3Impact.conclusion: v5.3 moved scores in right direction but strong remains 2-5 pts out of reach. The model is inherently conservative about 'strong' labels for competitive markets.
